title: Lecture Three Domain-Wall Edge-Mode Proof State
summary: Current proof-state ledger for the Lecture Three flagship theorem: the local Witten derivation is decomposed into bounded obligations, the parity-anomaly half level is split into a geometric hemisphere fragment plus a theorem-level response statement, the Callan-Harvey inflow-cancellation identity is explicit, the lecture-one boundary-mode precursor is explicit, and the remaining follow-up is narrower archival enrichment plus the separate continuum-versus-lattice scope boundary.
assumptions: The local continuum derivation and the cited-source fusion status must both stay visible.
regime: Proof-completion review state for the edge-mode theorem family.
scope: Ledger-level summary of the Lecture Three proof chain and its residual enrichment route.
representation: Proof-state ledger for the Lecture Three continuum edge-mode theorem family.
faithfulness_strategy: Treat this proof state only as a scaffold of obligations and scope boundaries; it stays faithful by pointing back to the trusted continuum theorem and by keeping the lattice no-go branch separate.
faithfulness_notes: This proof-state unit is not itself the theorem and should not absorb the continuum-versus-lattice distinction.
comparator_risks: A complete-looking scaffold can still blur the distinction between the continuum edge-mode theorem and the separate lattice no-go story.
attribution_requirements: Retain attribution to Witten for the lecture-local proof chain and to the cited Jackiw-Rebbi / Callan-Harvey sources for the neighboring source-grade ingredients.
admissible_assumptions: The proof state formalizes the local continuum theorem family only and keeps the lattice no-go theorem separate.
The cited-source archival enrichment remains outside the local formal scaffold.
formalization_blockers: The domain-wall zero-mode proof still depends on analytic lemmas that are not yet available as reusable Lean imports.
pass_conditions: The local continuum proof obligations are explicit and formula-bearing.
The Jackiw-Rebbi zero-mode and anomaly-inflow ingredients are explicit source-grade neighboring units.
The Callan-Harvey inflow-cancellation equation is explicit rather than only named abstractly.
The half-level parity-anomaly ingredient is factored into an explicit geometric Berry-flux fragment and a theorem-level response statement.
The proof state keeps the residual archival-enrichment follow-up and the anomaly-versus-lattice distinction visible.
failure_triggers: Fail if the local derivation is treated as identical to the lattice no-go proof.
Fail if the theorem is described as still lacking core Jackiw-Rebbi or anomaly-inflow source-grade units even though they are present.
Fail if anomaly inflow is invoked without the explicit bulk-plus-boundary cancellation equation.
Fail if the narrower archival-enrichment follow-up is erased.
retrieval_hints: Use when an answer or audit needs the full Lecture Three proof ledger.
